---
title: Contribute Data
---

AusTraits continues to integrate newly collected and legacy datasets, with future Zenodo releases planned. Contributions of any size can improve the resource for Australian plant science, conservation, and biodiversity infrastructure.

```{=html}
<div class="card-grid">
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Trait datasets</h3>
    <p>Share field, laboratory, literature-derived, or compiled trait data with accompanying metadata.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Legacy sources</h3>
    <p>Contribute transcriptions from reference books, classic works, taxonomic monographs, or archived datasets.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Expert review</h3>
    <p>Help improve trait definitions, allowable values, taxonomic alignment, and interpretation of specialised datasets.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Community reuse</h3>
    <p>Tell us how you use AusTraits so we can improve access pathways, documentation, and derived products.</p>
  </article>
</div>
```

**This project would not be possible without contributions from researchers across Australia and beyond. We are thankful for every contribution, no matter how large or small.**

To contribute data or discuss a possible collaboration, [contact the AusTraits team](mailto:austraits.database@gmail.com). See [Zenodo](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3568417) for a full list of researchers who have contributed data and expertise to AusTraits.
